threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think a 10-minute walk is far. Everyone here knows that's just a warm-up before the coffee shop.
The 196 bus rolled in at 3 AM, but the driver was chatting with a couple of locals about lost dogs. They didn't even ask for fare.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The day I heard a saxophonist jamming with a street artist, while a group of kids played football nearby—pure Neukölln magic.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: In Kreuzberg, you can find spontaneous street protests serving up free food. Just a slice of activism and community spirit that’s pure Berlin.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can tell by how they navigate the BART delays; natives roll with the punches, while newcomers just stare at their phones, confused.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy playing a trumpet near the Civic Center Muni stop. He stopped mid-song as the bus driver yelled about delays, then picked right back up.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists always think the secret to Gràcia's charm is the parks. They miss the evenings when locals gather at the tiny bars, sharing tapas and laughter.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a recent arrival by the way they order a 'café con leche' in the morning—far too loud and overly enthusiastic.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You queue at the coffee shop without knowing to order at the counter first. The locals know that's a rookie mistake.
Last night on the 59, a bloke was arguing with the driver about where to get off—turns out he missed his stop three times and was just too proud to admit it.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I realized I was part of Neukölln? When I saw a guy in a suit play chess with a street cat outside the Kiezkneipe.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg can you find a kebab shop doubling as a vinyl record store. Grab a döner while hunting for your next favorite album—it’s a dream combo.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can tell a local by their disdain for BART’s 'sudden' delays; we expect the chaos like fog rolling in. Tourists still seem shocked.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy juggling on the 21 bus yesterday while a lady was trying to sell incense. Only in SF do you get a live show with your commute.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is all about Gaudí, but the real charm's in the tiny, dusty bars where locals still drink vermut on a weekday afternoon.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell by the way they still ask for directions to the nearest Sagrada Família—like it’s a quick stroll.
